cases.rules_text:
1. Archive entry requires a recognized way into the archive and movement into the lower stack.
2. Record interference includes moving an unsealed dream page, rewriting a margin note, or placing a loose page under viewing glass.
3. A fading condition is present when a dream page begins to fade.
4. A repair-tool record requires carrying a repair bell and either ringing it or stamping a repair slip before closure.
5. Restorer status requires a night-restorer badge and a repair log at the blue desk.
6. Rescue transfer requires a fading dream page and either moving it to the safe shelf or covering it with a sleep cloth.
7. Visitor assistance requires guiding a lost visitor out while carrying the visitor rope.
8. The unregistered archive entry basis is present when there is archive entry.
9. The dream page intervention basis is present when archive entry is combined with record interference.
10. The fading archive damage basis is present when record interference concerns a fading page.
11. The restorer route authorization basis requires restorer status and a repair-tool record.
12. The emergency preservation authorization basis requires a rescue transfer and a repair-tool record.
13. The visitor escort allowance basis requires visitor assistance connected to archive entry.
14. The unregistered archive entry article covers unregistered archive entry basis; the prescribed consequence is quiet room warning.
15. The dream page intervention article covers dream page intervention basis; the prescribed consequence is page quarantine order.
16. The fading archive damage article covers fading archive damage basis; the prescribed consequence is memory shelf sealing.
17. The restorer route authorization article covers restorer route authorization basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
18. The emergency preservation authorization article covers emergency preservation authorization basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
19. The visitor escort allowance article covers visitor escort allowance basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
20. Where the dream page intervention article and the unregistered archive entry article both apply, the dream page intervention article prevails.
21. Where the fading archive damage article and the unregistered archive entry article both apply, the fading archive damage article prevails.
22. Where the fading archive damage article and the dream page intervention article both apply, the fading archive damage article prevails.
23. The restorer route authorization article operates as an exception to the unregistered archive entry article when both apply.
24. The restorer route authorization article operates as an exception to the dream page intervention article when both apply.
25. The emergency preservation authorization article operates as an exception to the dream page intervention article when both apply.
26. The emergency preservation authorization article operates as an exception to the fading archive damage article when both apply.
27. The visitor escort allowance article operates as an exception to the unregistered archive entry article when both apply.
cases.case_text: Avery unlocked the silent door of the dream archive. Avery followed the echo stair into the lower stack. No dream page began to fade. Avery did not carry a repair bell. Avery did not wear a night-restorer badge.

  assume defeated_assm: "defeated actor Art_Unregistered_Archive_Entry"
  then obtain higher where

cases.rules_text:
1. Archive entry requires a recognized way into the archive and movement into the lower stack.
2. Record interference includes moving an unsealed dream page, rewriting a margin note, or placing a loose page under viewing glass.
3. A fading condition is present when a dream page begins to fade.
4. A repair-tool record requires carrying a repair bell and either ringing it or stamping a repair slip before closure.
5. Restorer status requires a night-restorer badge and a repair log at the blue desk.
6. Rescue transfer requires a fading dream page and either moving it to the safe shelf or covering it with a sleep cloth.
7. Visitor assistance requires guiding a lost visitor out while carrying the visitor rope.
8. The unregistered archive entry basis is present when there is archive entry.
9. The dream page intervention basis is present when archive entry is combined with record interference.
10. The fading archive damage basis is present when record interference concerns a fading page.
11. The restorer route authorization basis requires restorer status and a repair-tool record.
12. The emergency preservation authorization basis requires a rescue transfer and a repair-tool record.
13. The visitor escort allowance basis requires visitor assistance connected to archive entry.
14. The unregistered archive entry article covers unregistered archive entry basis; the prescribed consequence is quiet room warning.
15. The dream page intervention article covers dream page intervention basis; the prescribed consequence is page quarantine order.
16. The fading archive damage article covers fading archive damage basis; the prescribed consequence is memory shelf sealing.
17. The restorer route authorization article covers restorer route authorization basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
18. The emergency preservation authorization article covers emergency preservation authorization basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
19. The visitor escort allowance article covers visitor escort allowance basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
20. Where the dream page intervention article and the unregistered archive entry article both apply, the dream page intervention article prevails.
21. Where the fading archive damage article and the unregistered archive entry article both apply, the fading archive damage article prevails.
22. Where the fading archive damage article and the dream page intervention article both apply, the fading archive damage article prevails.
23. The restorer route authorization article operates as an exception to the unregistered archive entry article when both apply.
24. The restorer route authorization article operates as an exception to the dream page intervention article when both apply.
25. The emergency preservation authorization article operates as an exception to the dream page intervention article when both apply.
26. The emergency preservation authorization article operates as an exception to the fading archive damage article when both apply.
27. The visitor escort allowance article operates as an exception to the unregistered archive entry article when both apply.
cases.case_text: Logan unlocked the silent door of the dream archive. Logan followed the echo stair into the lower stack. Logan moved an unsealed dream page from its shelf. The dream page began to fade. Logan carried a repair bell. Logan rang the repair bell before closure. Logan wore a night-restorer badge. Logan logged the repair at the blue desk. Logan moved the fading page to the safe shelf. Logan guided a lost visitor out of the lower stack. Logan carried the visitor rope while guiding the visitor. Logan did not stamp a repair slip before closure.

24. The restorer route authorization article operates as an exception to the dream page intervention article when both apply.
25. The emergency preservation authorization article operates as an exception to the dream page intervention article when both apply.
26. The emergency preservation authorization article operates as an exception to the fading archive damage article when both apply.
27. The visitor escort allowance article operates as an exception to the unregistered archive entry article when both apply.

8. The unregistered archive entry basis is present when there is archive entry.
9. The dream page intervention basis is present when archive entry is combined with record interference.
10. The fading archive damage basis is present when record interference concerns a fading page.
11. The restorer route authorization basis requires restorer status and a repair-tool record.
12. The emergency preservation authorization basis requires a rescue transfer and a repair-tool record.
13. The visitor escort allowance basis requires visitor assistance connected to archive entry.
14. The unregistered archive entry article covers unregistered archive entry basis; the prescribed consequence is quiet room warning.
15. The dream page intervention article covers dream page intervention basis; the prescribed consequence is page quarantine order.
16. The fading archive damage article covers fading archive damage basis; the prescribed consequence is memory shelf sealing.
17. The restorer route authorization article covers restorer route authorization basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
18. The emergency preservation authorization article covers emergency preservation authorization basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
19. The visitor escort allowance article covers visitor escort allowance basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
20. Where the dream page intervention article and the unregistered archive entry article both apply, the dream page intervention article prevails.
21. Where the fading archive damage article and the unregistered archive entry article both apply, the fading archive damage article prevails.
22. Where the fading archive damage article and the dream page intervention article both apply, the fading archive damage article prevails.
23. The restorer route authorization article operates as an exception to the unregistered archive entry article when both apply.
24. The restorer route authorization article operates as an exception to the dream page intervention article when both apply.
25. The emergency preservation authorization article operates as an exception to the dream page intervention article when both apply.
26. The emergency preservation authorization article operates as an exception to the fading archive damage article when both apply.
27. The visitor escort allowance article operates as an exception to the unregistered archive entry article when both apply.
cases.case_text: Rowan presented a gray reader card at the archive desk. Rowan entered the lower stack beyond that door. Rowan placed a loose dream page under viewing glass. The dream page began to fade. Rowan carried a repair bell. Rowan stamped a repair slip before closure. Rowan wore a night-restorer badge. Rowan logged the repair at the blue desk. Rowan did not ring the repair bell before closure.

theory dawe_case_30
  imports Conflict
begin

consts
  actor :: person

axiomatization where

and f11: "\<not> moves_unsealed_dream_page actor"
and f12: "\<not> rewrites_margin_note actor"

and f14: "\<not> moves_page_to_safe_shelf actor"
and f15: "\<not> covers_page_with_sleep_cloth actor"
and f16: "\<not> guides_lost_visitor_out actor"

lemma derive_archive_entry: "archive_entry actor"
  using f1 f2
  unfolding archive_entry_def

  using derive_archive_entry
  unfolding unregistered_archive_entry_basis_def
  by simp
lemma derive_fading_archive_damage_basis: "fading_archive_damage_basis actor"
  using derive_record_interference derive_fading_condition
  unfolding fading_archive_damage_basis_def
  by simp
lemma derive_restorer_route_authorization_basis: "restorer_route_authorization_basis actor"
  using derive_restorer_status derive_repair_tool_record

  by simp
lemma trigger_art_unregistered_archive_entry: "article_triggered Art_Unregistered_Archive_Entry actor"
  using derive_unregistered_archive_entry_basis

lemma trigger_art_fading_archive_damage: "article_triggered Art_Fading_Archive_Damage actor"

  unfolding overrides_def

lemma not_defeated_art_fading_archive_damage: "\<not> defeated actor Art_Fading_Archive_Damage"
proof
  assume defeated_assm: "defeated actor Art_Fading_Archive_Damage"
  then obtain higher where
    higher_triggered: "article_triggered higher actor"
    and higher_overrides: "overrides higher Art_Fading_Archive_Damage"

    using not_trigger_art_emergency_preservation_authorization

theorem final_result_no_liability: "final_no_liability actor"
  using applicable_art_restorer_route_authorization
